title: 3.0 TimpBsw and TimpHsw - How to classify

content.text: This page is to give an overview how to classify the module as TimpBsw or TimpHsw

rows: Base software components stack which sits on top of MCAL and CDDs Example: CDD_NM, LCM modules like SystemMgr, PLC/PSC (power state controller), RTMO etc.,
components like MCAL, I2Chandler, CDDs like Audio driver, Rtc driver
MCAL stack for TC3xx from Infineon

The I2cHandler is an abstraction for the low-level I2c driver and provides the APIs that are needed by higher-level components for performing I2C transactions. The I2C handler supports the handling of concurrent I2C transmit & receive requests that are requested by QM and ASIL components. The I2C handler abstracts from the physical I2C channels by providing virtual, hardware-independent communication channels. The I2C handler supports the handling of multiple I2C slave devices that are connected to the same physical I2C bus.
EcuAL.UartBuffer handler is a sw component that is wrapper of McAL.Uart component. It implements SW Tx and Rx queues on top of McAL.Uart. Both Rx and Tx queues are realized via ring buffers

AurixCoreDriver is a MCAL component which gives info for CPU modes. It provides APIs for disable and enable interrupts, also gives info for interrupt state. It also provides APIs for get and release spinlocks
McAL.SpiSlave is MCAL component which provides interfaces to transfer data as SPI slave using one of the Aurix2G QSPI modules
McAl.Stmr is a driver able to provide the current total 64-bit value of the system timer of a particular STMx within the controller

TempSensor_Aurix is a MCAL component which monitors Aurix internal temperature sensors (DTSC and DTS sensors). It provdes interfaces for reading the temeperature, getting and clearing the overflow and underflow limit of each sensor

The ContextAbstractionMcal component shall provide intefaces for Context Switching McAL Service ContextAbstractionMcal provide interfaces for DIO, GPT, ICU, McalLib, McuExt, Port, Pwm and SPI. These interfaces are used to switch context
McAL.Startup is a sw unit executed after the Boot firmware. The initialization sequence is followed as per Aurix startup application note
AdcController is a service component supporting time-controlled conversions of pre-configured groups of ADC-channels using McAL Adc-Driver in accordance to ASIL B.
Component for standby controller which handles Aurix Low Power Mode
Driver for Real Time clock ASIC. Give interfaces to set and get date and time. Also used to set alarm for STR wakeup
Audio stack which includes Audio driver, Manager and also MIC drivers
Component to handle read and write configuration data into KDS memory
Component which is used to log critical information in SCC side and transferred to SoC via INC and stored in UFS
Component to store fatal and critical information into NVM in SCC side and can be read via diagnostic command
Service component to record particular timestamp. Used mainly for Boot time record and log
Interface module to users who want to log infromation Interface to AR DLT stack via PDUR
The Context Switcher component performs the context switching with the help of services provided by the operating system
Help to log critical information during traps which will be handled via ProtectionHook
Hook implementation of Error, protection, shutdown, timing and Panic Hooks. Help to log critical logs for debug purpose and then reset via SSH
Component provides maximum , minumum and current system load and supporting measuring of stack usage per cores and tasks. Also, can measure CSA load (in latest Vcuplus platform)
Used in Fusion platform → need to check whether this component is needed
Component provides current system time and supporting conversion from ticks to microseconds and vice versa
Component which provides API like active and passive wait time
Implementation of Callback of EcuM and BswM. Also monitors the EcuM transition states using WdgM. Initial Sync of Master and slave cores
Implementation of EcuM Callbacks before start OS. Since EcuM is considered as ASIL context before starting the OS, this LCM module is also considered as ASIL component
Handles LCM related INC messages, extracts the INC message using protobuf and call specific LCM interface and also packs the message in case of transmit LCM data from SCC to SoC

Power Manager provides the possibility to execute actions in a ordered sequence in a specific configurable time intervals. The sequences could be configured and executed at any time, through interface or using OS event. During starting and shutting down of the component a powerup/powerdown sequences are executed, this provides the possibility to start drivers and peripherals in specific order
Power state controller called as PSC, will synchronize the startup and shutdown coordination with SoC during startup, shutdown and reset
System Manager provides mechanism to detect STR, 3 way shutdown of SoC etc.,
SystemStateController called SSC responsible for handling System states by processing different condition and change states.
Component to encode and decode the Protobuf message via iNC
Interface component for users to access INCTP stack
Interface component for third SPI INC channel for APA feature
IncTP core stack which handles Tx and Rx via SPI using Ring buffers and predefined INCTP protocol
Component used to transmit Com message from SCC to SOC and vice versa
Contains Component identifier for platform components
Components which implement Ring buffer logic and allows easy for other components to use ring buffer for data handling
Discuss with Diag team to move to Tools folder
Component which read temperature sensor ADC values, convert and send to SoC
